job.name: backup-db
job.description: Snapshot the live SQLite DB to data/backups/ using sqlite3 .backup (safe while app is running).

command.script: #!/bin/sh set -eu DB=/home/lab/app/src/data/irrigation.db BACKUP_DIR=/home/lab/app/src/data/backups mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR" ts=$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ) dest="$BACKUP_DIR/irrigation-$ts.db" # Python's sqlite3 is always available in the image. Use its backup API for a # consistent snapshot even if the app is writing. python3 -c " import sqlite3, sys src = sqlite3.connect('$DB') dst = sqlite3.connect('$dest') with dst: src.backup(dst) src.close(); dst.close() " echo "backup: $dest ($(stat -c %s "$dest") bytes)" # Keep last 10 backups. ls -1t "$BACKUP_DIR"/irrigation-*.db 2>/dev/null | tail -n +11 | xargs -r rm -v

errorhandler.script: #!/bin/sh # Alert on backup failure from the Rundeck node. TOKEN=$(rd keys get -p keys/pushover/app_token 2>/dev/null) USER_KEY=$(rd keys get -p keys/pushover/user_key 2>/dev/null) if [ -n "$TOKEN" ] && [ -n "$USER_KEY" ]; then curl -sf --max-time 10 \ -d "token=$TOKEN" \ -d "user=$USER_KEY" \ -d "title=Irrigation BACKUP FAILED" \ -d "message=The ops/backup-db job failed. Check Rundeck execution history for details." \ -d "priority=0" \ -d "sound=falling" \ https://api.pushover.net/1/messages.json fi
